decks.notes: The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to utilize a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to control the game and outvalue the opponent. The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through counterspells like Counterspell and Spell Snare, as well as removal spells like Fatal Push and Sheoldred's Edict. Card draw spells like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian help to ensure the player has access to their powerful control tools. The deck aims to establish dominance in the late game and win through attrition.

The deck utilizes the combination of Ral, Monsoon Mage and Glimpse the Impossible to generate massive storm counts and create a game-ending Grapeshot. By casting multiple spells in a single turn, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of powerful spells and burn effects. The various rituals and Manamorphose help to ramp up mana quickly while Wrenn's Resolve and Reckless Impulse provide card advantage. The deck also includes Past in Flames to recast spells from the graveyard and ensure the combo can be executed successfully.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Arboreal Grazer and Amulet of Vigor to cheat out powerful threats like Primeval Titan and Cultivator Colossus. By utilizing lands like Gruul Turf and Simic Growth Chamber, the deck can generate a large amount of mana to cast game-ending spells such as Scapeshift and Green Sun's Zenith. Additionally, cards like Urza's Saga and Hanweir Battlements provide additional utility and flexibility to the combo strategy. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with big creatures and efficient mana generation.

This deck focuses on using aggressive creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating value through card advantage. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board and protect its threats. The deck also has a strong synergy with Ephemerate, allowing it to reuse powerful enter-the-battlefield effects. With a mix of removal spells and efficient threats like Emperor of Bones and Overlord of the Balemurk, it aims to apply pressure on the opponent while disrupting their strategy.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifact creatures and various artifacts to quickly establish a board presence and overwhelm your opponent with aggressive plays. Emry, Lurker of the Loch allows for recursion of artifacts while Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary provide additional threats. Cards like Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble help generate additional artifacts while Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke provide control elements. Overall, this deck aims to quickly build a powerful board state while disrupting your opponent's game plan, leading to a swift victory through aggressive tactics.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Consign to Memory. The deck also utilizes powerful removal spells such as Solitude and Prismatic Ending to control the board. Quantum Riddler and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provide additional pressure and reach to close out games. The deck aims to apply pressure early and disrupt the opponent's strategy to secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on cycling through cards to fill up the graveyard with creatures. The goal is to cast Living End to bring back all the cycled creatures onto the battlefield at once, overwhelming the opponent with a sudden board presence. The deck also includes cards that can counter spells and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the key concept of the deck is to efficiently cycle through cards, control the game with counterspells, and then win with a huge board presence from Living End.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, while utilizing efficient burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to control the board and finish off opponents. The deck also includes card draw and filtering spells like Preordain and Expressive Iteration to maintain consistency and card advantage. Overall, it aims to apply early pressure and close out games swiftly through a combination of creature damage and direct burn spells.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize the Allosaurus Combo to quickly assemble the necessary pieces and win the game. By using cards like Summoner's Pact, Neoform, and Eldritch Evolution to search for key combo pieces such as Ureni, the Song Unending and Griselbrand, the deck aims to create a powerful board state that can overwhelm the opponent. Additionally, cards like Veil of Summer and Pact of Negation provide protection against disruption, while Boseiju, Who Endures helps ensure that important spells resolve. Overall, the deck focuses on efficiently executing its combo strategy to achieve victory.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts onto the battlefield to take advantage of the Affinity mechanic, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Urza's Saga provide value and recursion, while cards like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary put pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes control elements like Metallic Rebuke and Engineered Explosives to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to win the game quickly by leveraging the power of artifacts in an aggressive manner.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures that also disrupt opponents' game plans, forcing them to play on the back foot. They use cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to quickly put pressure on the opponent while using cards like Quantum Riddler and Consign to Memory to disrupt their strategy. The deck also has a few control elements like Prismatic Ending and Spell Snare to deal with threats. It aims to win quickly while keeping opponents off balance with disruptive plays.

The deck focuses on generating mana and drawing cards quickly in order to cast multiple spells in a single turn, ultimately leading to a powerful combo finish with cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot. It utilizes cards like Pyretic Ritual and Manamorphose to generate mana, while Reckless Impulse and Wrenn's Resolve help draw more cards to fuel the combo. The goal is to assemble the necessary pieces to cast Past in Flames and/or Glimpse the Impossible to recur spells and keep the combo going until the opponent is overwhelmed.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells while also incorporating a taxing element to slow down the opponent's game plan. By utilizing a combination of versatile removal spells and powerful creatures with evasion, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and disrupt the opponent's strategy, ultimately coming out on top through a combination of efficient threats and disruptive plays.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana with cards like Ruby Medallion and Manamorphose, to cast spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Glimpse the Impossible. This allows the player to chain together spells and combo off, using cards like Past in Flames to recur spells from the graveyard. The goal is to generate a large storm count, then finish off the opponent with a lethal Grapeshot. The deck also includes card draw and filtering with Reckless Impulse and Wish, as well as land fetching with cards like Wooded Foothills and Arid Mesa.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent quickly with a mix of efficient creatures and burn spells. Cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer allow for early aggression, while Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Goblin Bombardment provide additional reach to finish off opponents. Arena of Glory helps to protect the board and keep creatures alive, while Thraben Charm can provide utility as both removal and a combat trick. The deck aims to win the game by overwhelming the opponent with threats and burn damage.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature-based strategies combined with burn spells for direct damage. The deck aims to control the board with efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, while using burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to finish off opponents. Cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos provide additional utility and support for the aggressive game plan. The deck also includes lands like Arid Mesa and Sacred Foundry for consistent mana fixing to ensure a fast and powerful start to overwhelm opponents quickly.
